mnemonics: NoSQL = Not Only SQL. Think of it as a flexible friend who doesn't demand a strict schema for every interaction.
keyPoints: NoSQL databases are designed for scalability and flexible data models.
They are well-suited for handling large volumes of unstructured or semi-structured data.
Common types include document, key-value, column-family, and graph databases.
commonMistakes: Assuming NoSQL means no structure at all; data often has an implicit structure.
Using the wrong type of NoSQL database for a task (e.g., using a key-value store for complex relational queries).
